mappings.rationale: Covers production, distribution, trade, and the role of government in economic organization.
Covers analysis of costs, output, production functions, and factor markets.
Covers the organization of production in capitalist economies including corporations and market distribution.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationale: Directly covers commercial and central banking functions, monetary systems, and credit markets (subsection D.2).
Directly covers the keeping of accounts including financial statements, cost accounting, and measurement (subsection H.1).
Covers labour as an input including workforce deployment, employment, and the economic role of workers (subsection C.1).
Directly covers personnel administration, manpower planning, recruitment, and employee development (subsection H.3).
Covers the administration and control of production, business organization, and managerial functions (subsection H.4).
Covers the economic role of entrepreneurs in organizing production and business ventures (subsection C.1.j).
Covers business organization structures relevant to how firms organize production and distribution.
Covers the role of managers and leaders in organizing production and business enterprises.
Directly covers advertising as a marketing and distribution function (subsection I).
Covers the distribution of goods including marketing functions, retailing, and market research (subsection H.5).
Covers branding as part of marketing and merchandising in the distribution of goods.
Directly covers agricultural economics including farming organization, prices, and technology (subsection E).
Covers international trade, global distribution of resources, and transnational economic organization.
Covers international trade arrangements and regulatory frameworks for global commerce (subsection F.3).
Covers how business corporations address social responsibilities beyond profit maximization.
Covers technological innovation in production processes and the effect of technology on output.
Covers taxation as part of the government's role in production and distribution (subsection G.1).
Covers statistical methods used in economic analysis including price indexes and forecasting (subsection D.3).
Covers energy as an input in the productive process (subsection C.3).
Covers renewable energy sources as inputs in production, relevant to subsection C.3.
Covers risk assessment relevant to the distribution of risk and insurance (subsection J).
Covers financial mathematics used in capital markets, securities, and long-term investment (subsection D.2.g).
Covers historical development of trade, production, and distribution systems.
Covers the transformation of production organization from craft to industrial methods.
Covers the historical development of business organization, corporations, and production methods in America.
Covers foundational theories of production, trade, and the role of government in the economy.
Covers Keynesian theory of government spending, monetary policy, and their roles in production and distribution.
Covers project management as a form of production administration and control (subsection H.4).
Covers negotiation processes relevant to labour relations and commercial transactions.
Covers legal regulation of business consolidations, mergers, and competitive practices.
Covers economic development and the organization of production in developing economies.
Covers property rights over innovations and creative works relevant to business production and distribution.
